pattern: The constraints force a strict up-down alternation, so a direction-aware dynamic program counts how many sequences end at each value going up versus down, sped up with prefix sums.
hints: No two adjacent equal plus no three monotone means the sequence must strictly zigzag: every step reverses the previous direction.
Track, for each possible ending value, how many valid sequences end there with the last move being an increase, and separately with the last move being a decrease.
To extend an up-ending sequence you must go down to a strictly smaller value, and vice versa; prefix sums over the value axis make each length step linear.

edge_inputs.description: minimum length with a wider range

stress.description: modest length and range so the brute enumeration oracle stays cheap
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(3, 6)
    l = rng.randint(1, 50)
    width = rng.randint(1, 4)
    r = l + width
    return [n, l, r]
stress.note: range width <= 4 keeps the m^n brute count fast
